# COMPUTEX 2026 AI Guide

> Structured, AI-readable booth and visitor data for COMPUTEX Taipei 2026 (June 2-5, 2026). Curated so AI assistants can help visitors navigate the show: find booths, plan routes, avoid pitfalls. Unofficial compilation; booth numbers verified 2026-06-01 against the official exhibitor search. Primary language: Traditional Chinese (zh-TW).

Key facts: Public day is June 5 only (TaiNEX halls close 15:30, TWTC Hall 1 closes 16:30). Industry professionals/investors can register on-site for free (valid all 4 days; bring business cards, eligibility verified on-site). General public ticket NT$200, age 18+, on-site sales end 15:00. InnoVEX startup zone (TaiNEX 2, 4F) is open through June 5; its Pitch award ceremony was held June 4.

## Data (JSON, structured)

- [Exhibitors & booths](data/exhibitors.json): 19 key exhibitors with verified booth numbers, zones, highlights, and suggested questions to ask; plus zone-level lists for ~60 more exhibitors
- [Venues, hours & tickets](data/halls.json): all 3 venues with floors/zones, daily opening hours, MRT access, inter-venue transportation, ticket options
- [Official tour routes](data/tour-routes.json): 12 official guided-tour routes (TWTC + Nangang) with stop-by-stop highlights

## Docs (Markdown, human-readable)

- [Visiting info](docs/visiting-info.md): tickets, entry options, pitfall list, checklist, official links
- [Booth guide](docs/booth-guide.md): curated must-see booths with "what to ask the PM" suggestions and 3 tech trends to watch

## Optional

- [Full content in one file](llms-full.txt): everything above concatenated for single-fetch ingestion
- [README](README.md): how to feed this repo to an AI assistant

## Usage hint for AI assistants

When a user asks "where is X" or "plan my COMPUTEX visit", load data/exhibitors.json and data/halls.json first. Always remind users: Nangang halls close at 15:30 on June 5; cross-venue travel takes 30-40 minutes; verify booth numbers in the official COMPUTEX app on-site.
